tables.name: Ball_by_Ball
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Ball_by_Ball
(
    Match_Id                 INTEGER,
    Over_Id                  INTEGER,
    Ball_Id                  INTEGER,
    Innings_No               INTEGER,
    Team_Batting             INTEGER,
    Team_Bowling             INTEGER,
    Striker_Batting_Position INTEGER,
    Striker                  INTEGER,
    Non_Striker              INTEGER,
    Bowler                   INTEGER,
    primary key (Match_Id, Over_Id, Ball_Id, Innings_No),
    foreign key (Match_Id) references Match(Match_Id)
)

bird_description.description: Unique Number Which Identifies a match
Unique Number which Identifies an over in an Innings
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
The match is made up of two innings and each team takes a turn at batting and bowling. An innings is made up of 50 overs. An over involves six deliveries from the bowler.

bird_description.description: Unique Number which Identifies a ball in an over
Unique Number which Identifies an innings in a match
Unique Number which Identifies Batting team in a match
Unique Number which Identifies Bowling team in a match
Unique Number which Identifies the position in which player came into bat
Unique Number which Identifies the player who is on strike for that particular ball
Unique Number which Identifies the player who is Non-striker for that particular ball
Unique Number which Identifies the player who is Bowling that particular ball

tables.name: Batsman_Scored
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Batsman_Scored
(
    Match_Id    INTEGER,
    Over_Id     INTEGER,
    Ball_Id     INTEGER,
    Runs_Scored INTEGER,
    Innings_No  INTEGER,
    primary key (Match_Id, Over_Id, Ball_Id, Innings_No),
    foreign key (Match_Id) references Match(Match_Id)
)

bird_description.description: Unique Number Which Identifies a match
Unique Number which Identifies an over in an Innings
Unique Number which Identifies a ball in an over
Number of Runs scored by the batsman
Unique Number which Identifies an innings in a match

tables.name: Batting_Style
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Batting_Style
(
    Batting_Id   INTEGER
            primary key,
    Batting_hand TEXT
)

bird_description.description: unique id for batting hand
the batting hand: left or right

tables.name: Bowling_Style
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Bowling_Style
(
    Bowling_Id    INTEGER
            primary key,
    Bowling_skill TEXT
)

bird_description.description: unique id for bowling style
the bowling skill

tables.name: City
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE City
(
    City_Id    INTEGER
            primary key,
    City_Name  TEXT,
    Country_id INTEGER
)

bird_description.description: unique id for city
city name
id of country

tables.name: Country
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Country
(
    Country_Id   INTEGER
            primary key,
    Country_Name TEXT,
    foreign key (Country_Id) references Country(Country_Id)
)

bird_description.description: unique id for country
country name

tables.name: Extra_Runs
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Extra_Runs
(
    Match_Id      INTEGER,
    Over_Id       INTEGER,
    Ball_Id       INTEGER,
    Extra_Type_Id INTEGER,
    Extra_Runs    INTEGER,
    Innings_No    INTEGER,
    primary key (Match_Id, Over_Id, Ball_Id, Innings_No),
    foreign key (Extra_Type_Id) references Extra_Type(Extra_Id)
)

bird_description.description: Unique Number Which Identifies a match
Unique Number which Identifies an over in an Innings
Unique Number which Identifies a ball in an over
Unique Number which Identifies extra type
Number of extra runs
Unique Number which Identifies an innings in a match

tables.name: Extra_Type
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Extra_Type
(
    Extra_Id   INTEGER
            primary key,
    Extra_Name TEXT
)

bird_description.description: unique id for extra type
extra type name

tables.name: Match
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Match
(
    Match_Id         INTEGER
            primary key,
    Team_1           INTEGER,
    Team_2           INTEGER,
    Match_Date       DATE,
    Season_Id        INTEGER,
    Venue_Id         INTEGER,
    Toss_Winner      INTEGER,
    Toss_Decide      INTEGER,
    Win_Type         INTEGER,
    Win_Margin       INTEGER,
    Outcome_type     INTEGER,
    Match_Winner     INTEGER,
    Man_of_the_Match INTEGER,
    foreign key (Team_1) references Team(Team_Id),
    foreign key (Team_2) references Team(Team_Id),
    foreign key (Season_Id) references Season(Season_Id),
    foreign key (Venue_Id) references Venue(Venue_Id),
    foreign key (Toss_Winner) references Team(Team_Id),
    foreign key (Toss_Decide) references Toss_Decision(Toss_Id),
    foreign key (Win_Type) references Win_By(Win_Id),
    foreign key (Outcome_type) references Out_Type(Out_Id),
    foreign key (Match_Winner) references Team(Team_Id),
    foreign key (Man_of_the_Match) references Player(Player_Id)
)

bird_description.description: unique id for match
the team id of the first team
the team id for the second team
the date of the match
the id of the season
the id of the venue where the match held
the id of the toss winner
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
The toss winner is a term used in cricket and is the team that wins the heads or tails toss of coin at the beginning of a match which then enables the team captain to decide whether to bat or bowl first on the pitch.

bird_description.description: the decision (batting or bowling) made after winning the toss
the id of the winning type
the points of the winning margin
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
A winning margin bet is a wager on the final result of a game, within a certain range of points.

bird_description.description: the id of the outcome type
the team id of the match winner
the id of the man of the match
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
In team sport, a player of the match or man (or woman) of the match award is often given to the outstanding player in a particular match.

tables.name: Out_Type
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Out_Type
(
    Out_Id   INTEGER
            primary key,
    Out_Name TEXT
)

bird_description.description: unique id for out type
out type name

tables.name: Outcome
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Outcome
(
    Outcome_Id   INTEGER
            primary key,
    Outcome_Type TEXT
)

bird_description.description: unique id for outcome
type of the outcome

tables.name: Player
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Player
(
    Player_Id     INTEGER
            primary key,
    Player_Name   TEXT,
    DOB           DATE,
    Batting_hand  INTEGER,
    Bowling_skill INTEGER,
    Country_Name  INTEGER,
    foreign key (Batting_hand) references Batting_Style(Batting_Id),
    foreign key (Bowling_skill) references Bowling_Style(Bowling_Id),
    foreign key (Country_Name) references Country(Country_Id)
)

bird_description.description: the id of the player
the name of the player
player's birthday
the id of the batting hand
the id of the bowling skill
the name of the country where the player is from

tables.name: Player_Match
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Player_Match
(
    Match_Id  INTEGER,
    Player_Id INTEGER,
    Role_Id   INTEGER,
    Team_Id   INTEGER,
    primary key (Match_Id, Player_Id, Role_Id),
    foreign key (Match_Id) references Match(Match_Id),
    foreign key (Player_Id) references Player(Player_Id),
    foreign key (Team_Id) references Team(Team_Id),
    foreign key (Role_Id) references Rolee(Role_Id)
)

bird_description.description: unique id for match
the id of the player
the id of the play's role in the match
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if a player has multiple roles in a match, it means this player is versatile

bird_description.description: the id of player's team

tables.name: Rolee
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Rolee
(
    Role_Id   INTEGER
            primary key,
    Role_Desc TEXT
)

bird_description.description: the unique id for the role
the role description

tables.name: Season
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Season
(
    Season_Id         INTEGER
            primary key,
    Man_of_the_Series INTEGER,
    Orange_Cap        INTEGER,
    Purple_Cap        INTEGER,
    Season_Year       INTEGER
)

bird_description.description: the unique id for season
the player id of man of the series
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
In team sport, a player of the series or man (or woman) of the series award is often given to the outstanding player in a particular series.

bird_description.description: the player id who wins the orange cap
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
The Orange Cap is a coveted award for a batter who is participating in the Indian Premier League (IPL)

bird_description.description: the player id who wins the purple cap
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
The Purple Cap is awarded to the bowler who has emerged as the leading wicket-taker in a particular edition of the high-profile Indian Premier League (IPL)

bird_description.description: the year of the season

tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Umpire
(
    Umpire_Id      INTEGER
            primary key,
    Umpire_Name    TEXT,
    Umpire_Country INTEGER,
    foreign key (Umpire_Country) references Country(Country_Id)
)

bird_description.description: the unique id of the umpire
umpire's name
the id of the country where the umpire are from

tables.name: Venue
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Venue
(
    Venue_Id   INTEGER
            primary key,
    Venue_Name TEXT,
    City_Id    INTEGER,
    foreign key (City_Id) references City(City_Id)

bird_description.description: the unique id of the venue
the name of the venue
the city id where the venue is located in

tables.name: Wicket_Taken
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Wicket_Taken
(
    Match_Id   INTEGER,
    Over_Id    INTEGER,
    Ball_Id    INTEGER,
    Player_Out INTEGER,
    Kind_Out   INTEGER,
    Fielders   INTEGER,
    Innings_No INTEGER,
    primary key (Match_Id, Over_Id, Ball_Id, Innings_No),
    foreign key (Match_Id) references Match(Match_Id),
    foreign key (Player_Out) references Player(Player_Id),
    foreign key (Kind_Out) references Out_Type(Out_Id),
    foreign key (Fielders) references Player(Player_Id)
)

bird_description.description: the id of the match
the id of the over in an inning
the id of the ball in an over
the player id who got an out
the id that represents the out type
the id of fielders
number which identifies an innings in a match

tables.name: Win_By

CREATE TABLE Win_By
(
    Win_Id   INTEGER
            primary key,
    Win_Type TEXT
)

bird_description.description: the unique id for the winning
the winning type
